factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue growth was a result of new business, the penetration of additional products and services into existing customers and price increases, partially offset by lost business.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2022

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin segments, improving gross margins.

evidence.text: The improvement in gross margin from the third quarter of the prior fiscal year was primarily driven by favorable changes in the sales mix, as well as improved leverage of fixed costs.

factors.summary: The fastest growing end markets are the First Aid and Safety Services, with strong demand across customer segments.

evidence.text: First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased 8.8%, driven by new business, product penetration, and customer retention.

factors.summary: Input costs are rising, but the company is partially offsetting them through pricing and productivity improvements.

evidence.text: Cost of uniform rental and facility services increased $73.9 million, or 8.9%, for the three months ended February 28, 2023, compared to the three months ended February 28, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2022

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with improvements in selling and administrative expenses.

evidence.text: Selling and administrative expenses as a percent of revenue were 26.8% for the three months ended February 28, 2023, compared to 25.0% for the same period in the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2022

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share through strategic acquisitions and increased customer penetration.

evidence.text: We evaluate operating segment performance based on revenue and income before income taxes. Revenue and income before income taxes for the three and nine months ended February 28, 2023 and 2022, for the two reportable operating segments and All Other are presented in Note 11 entitled Segment Information of 'Notes to Consolidated Condensed Financial Statements.'

factors.summary: The competitive environment is stable, with the company focusing on differentiation through customer relationships and product offerings.

evidence.text: Cintas’ principal objective is 'to exceed customers’ expectations in order to maximize the long-term value of Cintas for shareholders and working partners,' and it provides the framework and focus for Cintas’ business strategy.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving, supported by strong operating cash flows and efficient working capital management.

evidence.text: Net cash provided by operating activities was $1,044.2 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2023, compared to $987.1 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2022. The change from the prior fiscal year was primarily due to an increase in net income and favorable changes in working capital, specifically current income taxes, accrued liabilities and accounts payable.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are active, with significant share buybacks and consistent dividend payments.

evidence.text: From the inception of the July 27, 2021 share buyback program through February 28, 2023, Cintas purchased a total of 2.7 million shares of Cintas common stock at an average price of $385.62 per share for a total purchase price of $1.0 billion. The Board of Directors declared dividends totaling $332.4 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2023.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are increasing, reflecting investments in growth opportunities across segments.

evidence.text: Capital expenditures were $224.1 million and $165.9 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2023 and 2022, respectively. Capital expenditures in the nine months ended February 28, 2023, included $155.9 million for the Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment and $47.6 million for the First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment. The increase in capital expenditures during the nine months ended February 28, 2023 over the same period in the prior fiscal year is due to an investment in the operating segments to support continued market penetration and revenue growth.

factors.summary: Debt leverage is stable, with manageable interest costs and access to liquidity through credit facilities.

evidence.text: Cintas has access to $2.0 billion of debt capacity from its amended and restated revolving credit facility. As of February 28, 2023, there was $199.0 million of commercial paper outstanding with a weighted average interest rate of 4.79% and maturity dates less than 90 days and no borrowings on our revolving credit facility. Cintas was in compliance with all of the debt covenants for all periods presented.

factors.summary: Acquisition activity is ongoing, with purchases in multiple segments to support growth.

evidence.text: Cash paid for acquisitions of businesses was $33.0 million and $150.8 million for the nine months ended February 28, 2023 and 2022, respectively. The acquisitions during both the nine months ended February 28, 2023 and 2022, occurred in our Uniform Rental and Facility Services reportable operating segment, our First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment and our Fire Protection operating segment, which is included in All Other.

factors.summary: The economic environment is a tailwind, with strong cash flows and growth opportunities supporting the business.

evidence.text: Cash flows provided by operating activities have historically supplied us with a significant source of liquidity. We generally use these cash flows to fund most, if not all, of our operations and expansion activities and dividends on our common stock. We may also use cash flows provided by operating activities, as well as proceeds from long-term debt and short-term borrowings, to fund growth and expansion opportunities, as well as other cash requirements such as the repurchase of our common stock and payment of long-term debt.

factors.summary: Litigation exposure is not material, with no significant impact on financial position, results, or cash flows.

evidence.text: Cintas is subject to legal proceedings, insurance receipts, legal settlements and claims arising from the ordinary course of its business... In the opinion of management, the aggregate liability, if any, with respect to such ordinary course of business actions will not have a material adverse effect on the consolidated financial position, consolidated results of operations or consolidated cash flows of Cintas.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating across multiple segments, with notable increases in both the reportable operating segments and other revenue. The First Aid and Safety Services segment showed a strong 12.8% organic revenue growth, while other segments also demonstrated significant growth rates.

evidence.text: The organic revenue growth rate for the reportable operating segment was 10.8%. The organic revenue growth rate for other revenue was 15.8%. Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 11.4%. Other revenue organic growth was 18.4%
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2022

evidence.text: Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 12.8%. First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue was positively impacted by 1.6% due to acquisitions and negatively impacted by 0.2% due to foreign currency exchange rate fluctuations.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022

factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, driven by both new business and increased customer penetration, as well as strong revenue growth in the First Aid and Safety Services segment.

evidence.text: Total revenue increased 11.7% to $2,190.0 million for the three months ended February 28, 2023, compared to $1,960.5 million for the three months ended February 28, 2022.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended February 28, 2022

evidence.text: First Aid and Safety Services reportable operating segment revenue increased from $614.2 million to $701.7 million, or 14.2%, for the nine months ended February 28, 2023, over the same period in the prior fiscal year. Organic revenue growth for this reportable operating segment was 12.8%.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by efficiency improvements, cost leverage, favorable sales mix, and improved leverage of fixed costs.

evidence.text: Gross margin as a percentage of revenue was 51.6% for the quarter ended February 28, 2023, compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 44.2% in the same period of the prior fiscal year.

The gross margin as a percent of revenue was 50.5% for the nine months ended February 28, 2023, which was an increase of 630 basis points compared to the gross margin as a percent of revenue of 44.2% in the same period of the prior fiscal year. The change in gross margin from the same period of the prior fiscal year was primarily driven by favorable changes in the sales mix as well as improved leverage of fixed costs.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended February 28, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended February 28, 2022
